html:
Responsible AI deployment requires governance structures that go beyond technical audits. Key frameworks include:

  • The EU AI Act: a risk-based regulatory framework classifying AI systems by potential harm.
  • Algorithmic impact assessments: structured processes for identifying risks before deployment.
  • Contestability mechanisms: how affected individuals can challenge AI decisions.

Unit 1 introduced the principles. Unit 2 showed that fairness criteria conflict mathematically. Unit 3 asks: given those constraints, who should decide which tradeoffs to make, and through what process?

Discussion prompt: Should AI governance be handled by technical standards bodies, governments, or affected communities? What are the tradeoffs of each approach?
